sentences.sentence: Many Scientists believe that socialization takes a long process, while compliance is the outset of it.
sentences.translation: 许多科学家认为，社会化是一个漫长的过程，而服从则是这一过程的起点。

structure.text: Many Scientists believe
that socialization takes a long process
while compliance is the outset of it

difficult_points: that 引导 believe 的宾语从句，说明科学家所持的观点。
while 在此表示对比，it 指代 socialization 这一过程。

sentences.sentence: Accordingly, compliance for the education of children is the priority.
sentences.translation: 因此，在儿童教育中，培养服从意识是首要任务。

structure.text: Accordingly
compliance for the education of children
is the priority

difficult_points: Accordingly 承接上文，表示由前述观点推出结论。
for the education of children 修饰 compliance，限定其所处的教育语境。

sentences.sentence: Motivationally distinct forms of child compliance, mutually positive affect, and maternal control, observed in 3 control contexts in 103 dyads of mothers and their 26-41-month-old children, were examined as correlates of internalization, assessed using observations of children while alone with prohibited temptations and maternal ratings.
sentences.translation: 研究人员考察了动机不同的儿童服从形式、母子间相互的积极情感以及母亲的控制；这些因素是在103对母亲及其26至41个月大孩子的三种控制情境中观察到的，并被作为内化的相关因素，而内化程度则通过观察孩子独自面对被禁止的诱惑时的表现及母亲评分来评估。

structure.text: Motivationally distinct forms of child compliance, mutually positive affect, and maternal control
observed in 3 control contexts in 103 dyads of mothers and their 26-41-month-old children
were examined as correlates of internalization
assessed using observations of children while alone with prohibited temptations and maternal ratings

difficult_points: 主语由三个并列研究变量构成，中心谓语直到 were examined 才出现。
observed... 修饰三个研究变量，而句末 assessed... 修饰 internalization，两个过去分词短语层级不同。
while alone 省略 children were，表示孩子独处且面对禁物诱惑时。

sentences.sentence: One form of compliance (committed compliance), when the child appeared committed wholeheartedly to the maternal agenda and eager to endorse and accept it, was emphasized.
sentences.translation: 研究尤其强调了一种服从形式——投入式服从，即孩子表现出全心接受母亲的安排，并愿意认可和接受它。

structure.text: One form of compliance (committed compliance)
when the child appeared committed wholeheartedly to the maternal agenda and eager to endorse and accept it
was emphasized

difficult_points: 括号中的 committed compliance 是对 one form of compliance 的同位说明。
when 从句解释这种服从出现时的具体表现，it 指 maternal agenda。

sentences.sentence: Mother-child mutually positive affect was both a predictor and a concomitant of committed compliance.
sentences.translation: 母子之间相互的积极情感既能预测投入式服从，也会与之相伴出现。

structure.text: Mother-child mutually positive affect
was
both a predictor and a concomitant of committed compliance

difficult_points: both...and... 连接两个并列表语 predictor 和 concomitant。
of committed compliance 同时说明预测和伴随的对象。

expressions.text: both a predictor and a concomitant of
expressions.meaning_zh: 既是……的预测因素，又与……相伴

sentences.sentence: Children who shared positive effect with their mothers showed a high level of committed compliance and were also more internalized.
sentences.translation: 与母亲共享积极情感的孩子表现出较高水平的投入式服从，其行为规范也内化得更好。

structure.text: Children
who shared positive effect with their mothers
showed a high level of committed compliance and were also more internalized

difficult_points: who 引导定语从句，修饰 Children。
showed 与 were 是并列谓语；more internalized 指行为规范被孩子内化的程度更高。

sentences.sentence: Differences and similarities between children's compliance with requests and prohibitions ("Do"vs. "Don't" demand contexts) were also explored.
sentences.translation: 研究还探讨了儿童服从请求与服从禁令（即“做”与“不要做”两类要求情境）之间的差异与相似之处。

structure.text: Differences and similarities
between children's compliance with requests and prohibitions ("Do"vs. "Don't" demand contexts)
were also explored

difficult_points: between...and... 比较的是对 requests 和 prohibitions 两种要求的服从。
括号把两类情境概括为“Do”与“Don't”；原文引号与 vs. 之间排版紧密。

sentences.sentence: Maternal "Dos" appeared more challenging to toddlers than the "Don'ts."
sentences.translation: 对幼儿而言，母亲要求他们“做某事”似乎比要求他们“不要做某事”更具挑战性。

structure.text: Maternal "Dos"
appeared more challenging
to toddlers than the "Don'ts."

difficult_points: Dos 和 Don'ts 名词化，分别指肯定性请求与禁止性要求。
more...than... 构成比较，to toddlers 说明感到困难的是幼儿。

sentences.sentence: Some individual coherence of behavior was also found across both demand contexts.
sentences.translation: 研究还发现，个体在这两类要求情境中的行为存在一定一致性。

structure.text: Some individual coherence of behavior
was also found
across both demand contexts

difficult_points: coherence 在此指同一孩子跨情境表现出的行为一致性。
across both demand contexts 表示这种一致性横跨前述两类要求情境。

sentences.sentence: The implication of committed compliance for emerging internalized regulators of conduct is discussed.
sentences.translation: 文章讨论了投入式服从对于正在形成的内在行为调节机制有何意义。

structure.text: The implication of committed compliance
for emerging internalized regulators of conduct
is discussed

difficult_points: of committed compliance 修饰 implication，说明所讨论的是哪一现象的意义。
emerging internalized regulators of conduct 指儿童逐渐形成的内在行为规范与调节机制。

sentences.sentence: A number of parents were not easy to be aware of the compliance, some even overlooked their children's noncompliance.
sentences.translation: 不少父母不容易意识到服从问题，有些人甚至忽视了孩子的不服从行为。

structure.text: A number of parents were not easy to be aware of the compliance
some even overlooked their children's noncompliance

difficult_points: 逗号直接连接两个独立分句，属于原文的逗号拼接。
were not easy to be aware of 搭配不自然，按语意理解为父母“不容易意识到”。

sentences.sentence: Despite good education, these children did not follow the words from their parents on several occasion, especially boys in certain ages.
sentences.translation: 尽管受过良好教育，这些孩子仍有几次不听父母的话，某些年龄段的男孩尤其如此。

structure.text: Despite good education
these children did not follow the words from their parents
on several occasion, especially boys in certain ages

difficult_points: Despite 后接名词短语表示让步，相当于“尽管受过良好教育”。
原文 several occasion 和 in certain ages 存在数与介词搭配问题，翻译按语意处理。

sentences.sentence: Fortunately, this rate was acceptable, some parents could be patient with the noncompliance.
sentences.translation: 所幸，这种不服从的发生率尚可接受，一些父母也能耐心面对。

structure.text: Fortunately
this rate was acceptable
some parents could be patient with the noncompliance

difficult_points: this rate 承接上句，指孩子不听从父母的发生频率。
两个独立分句只用逗号连接，是原文的逗号拼接。

sentences.sentence: Someone held that noncompliance is probably not a wrong thing.
sentences.translation: 有人认为，不服从或许并不是坏事。

structure.text: Someone held
that noncompliance is probably not a wrong thing

difficult_points: that 从句作 held 的宾语，陈述所持观点。
held 在此是“持有某种看法”，不是“拿住”。

sentences.sentence: In order to determine the effects of different parental disciplinary techniques on young children's compliance and noncompliance, mothers were trained to observe emotional incidents involving their own toddler-aged children.
sentences.translation: 为了确定不同父母管教方式对幼儿服从与不服从行为的影响，研究人员训练母亲观察涉及自己幼儿的情绪事件。

structure.text: In order to determine the effects of different parental disciplinary techniques on young children's compliance and noncompliance
mothers were trained
to observe emotional incidents involving their own toddler-aged children

difficult_points: 句首 In order to... 是目的状语，effects 后的 on 引出受影响的对象。
to observe 补充说明母亲被训练去做什么，involving... 是现在分词修饰 incidents。

sentences.sentence: Reports of disciplinary encounters were analyzed in terms of the types of discipline used (reasoning, verbal prohibition, physical coercion, love withdrawal, and combinations thereof) and children's responses to that discipline (compliance/ noncompliance and avoidance).
sentences.translation: 研究按照所采用的管教类型（说理、口头禁止、身体强制、撤回关爱及其组合）以及儿童对管教的反应（服从／不服从和回避）来分析管教事件报告。

structure.text: Reports of disciplinary encounters
were analyzed

in terms of the types of discipline used (reasoning, verbal prohibition, physical coercion, love withdrawal, and combinations thereof) and children's responses to that discipline (compliance/ noncompliance and avoidance)

difficult_points: in terms of 引出分析所依据的两个并列维度：管教类型和儿童反应。
used 是过去分词修饰 discipline；thereof 指前述管教方式的各种组合。

sentences.sentence: The relation between compliance/ noncompliance and type of misdeed (harm to persons, harm to property, and lapses of self-control) was also analyzed.
sentences.translation: 研究还分析了服从／不服从与过失类型（伤害他人、损坏财物和自控失误）之间的关系。

structure.text: The relation between compliance/ noncompliance and type of misdeed (harm to persons, harm to property, and lapses of self-control)
was also analyzed

difficult_points: between...and... 连接服从状态与过失类型两个变量。
括号列举三类 misdeed，其中 lapses of self-control 指一时失去自控。

sentences.sentence: Results indicated that love withdrawal combined with other techniques was most effective in securing children's compliance and that its effectiveness was not a function of the type of technique with which it was combined.
sentences.translation: 结果表明，撤回关爱与其他方式结合时，最能促使儿童服从，而且其效果并不取决于与它搭配的是哪一种管教方式。

structure.text: Results indicated
that love withdrawal combined with other techniques was most effective in securing children's compliance
and that its effectiveness was not a function of the type of technique with which it was combined

difficult_points: 两个 that 从句由 and 并列，共同作 indicated 的宾语。
combined with other techniques 是过去分词修饰 love withdrawal。
with which it was combined 是介词前置的定语从句，which 指 type of technique，it 指 love withdrawal。

sentences.sentence: Avoidant responses and affective reunification with the parent were more likely to follow love withdrawal than any other technique.
sentences.translation: 与其他任何管教方式相比，撤回关爱之后更容易出现回避反应以及孩子与父母在情感上的重新亲近。

structure.text: Avoidant responses and affective reunification with the parent
were more likely to follow love withdrawal
than any other technique

difficult_points: follow 在此表示“随后发生”，不是“听从”。
more likely...than... 比较撤回关爱与其他管教方式之后出现这些反应的概率。

sentences.sentence: Physical coercion was somewhat less effective than love withdrawal, while reasoning and verbal prohibition were not at all effective except when both were combined with physical coercion.
sentences.translation: 身体强制的效果略逊于撤回关爱；而说理和口头禁止本身完全无效，只有两者都与身体强制结合时才有效。

structure.text: Physical coercion was somewhat less effective than love withdrawal
while reasoning and verbal prohibition were not at all effective
except when both were combined with physical coercion

difficult_points: while 引出与前半句对比的另一项研究结果。
except when 给出例外条件，both 指 reasoning and verbal prohibition。

sentences.sentence: "Noncompliant Children sometimes prefer to say to directly as they were younger, they are easy to deal with the relationship with contemporaries.
sentences.translation: “不服从的孩子年幼时有时更喜欢直接说出来；他们较容易处理与同龄人的关系。

structure.text: Noncompliant Children sometimes prefer to say to directly as they were younger
they are easy to deal with the relationship with contemporaries

difficult_points: 本句以引号开头但引语尚未结束，且两个独立分句仅以逗号连接。
say to directly 缺少宾语且语序异常；后半句 easy to deal with 的主客体搭配也不规范，翻译按上下文推断。

sentences.sentence: When they are growing up.
sentences.translation: 当他们逐渐长大时。

structure.text: When they are growing up

difficult_points: When 引导的时间从句本应依附主句，但原文在此用句号截断。
该残句语意与下一句衔接，表示孩子成长期间的变化。

sentences.sentence: During the period that children are getting elder, who may learn to use more advanced approaches for their noncompliance.
sentences.translation: 在孩子年龄增长的过程中，他们可能会学会用更成熟的方式表达不服从。

structure.text: During the period that children are getting elder
who may learn to use more advanced approaches for their noncompliance

difficult_points: 句首 During... 是时间状语，但其后 who 从句缺少明确先行词，原句没有完整主句。
getting elder 搭配不自然，按“逐渐长大”理解；approaches for their noncompliance 指表达不服从的方式。

sentences.sentence: They are more skillful to negotiate or give reasons for refusal rather than show their opposite idea to parents directly."
sentences.translation: 他们更善于协商或说明拒绝的理由，而不是直接向父母表达反对意见。”

structure.text: They are more skillful
to negotiate or give reasons for refusal
rather than show their opposite idea to parents directly

difficult_points: rather than 与前面的 to negotiate or give reasons 对比，后面省略重复的 to。
句末引号关闭从 C-01 开始的跨句引语；opposite idea 表达不自然，意为反对意见。

sentences.sentence:
Said Henry Porter, a scholar working in
Psychology Institute of UK.
sentences.translation: 英国心理研究所学者亨利·波特说道。

structure.text: Said Henry Porter
a scholar working in Psychology Institute of UK

difficult_points: Said Henry Porter 是引语后的倒装式报道语，但独立成句且首字母大写，属于原文排版。
working in... 是现在分词修饰 scholar；原文在 in 后跨行，JSON 保留该换行。

sentences.sentence: He indicated that noncompliance means growth in some way, may have benefit for children.
sentences.translation: 他指出，不服从在某种意义上意味着成长，可能会对孩子有益。

structure.text: He indicated
that noncompliance means growth in some way
may have benefit for children

difficult_points: that 从句说明 indicated 的内容，in some way 表示“在某种意义上”。
逗号后的 may have benefit 缺少显式主语，按语意仍以 noncompliance 为主语。

sentences.sentence: Many Experts held different viewpoints in recent years, they tried drilling compliance into children.
sentences.translation: 近年来，许多专家持有不同观点，他们试图把服从观念灌输给孩子。

structure.text: Many Experts held different viewpoints in recent years
they tried drilling compliance into children

difficult_points: 两个独立分句只由逗号连接，是原文的逗号拼接。
drill...into... 在此是比喻用法，表示反复灌输。

sentences.sentence: His collaborator Wallace Friesen believed that Organizing a child's daily activities so that they occur in the same order each day as much as possible.
sentences.translation: 他的合作者华莱士·弗里森认为，应尽可能安排孩子每天按相同顺序进行日常活动。

structure.text: His collaborator Wallace Friesen believed
that Organizing a child's daily activities
so that they occur in the same order each day as much as possible

difficult_points: that 后以动名词 Organizing 开始，却缺少谓语，原文宾语从句在语法上不完整。
so that 引出安排活动的目的，they 指 daily activities；翻译按建议含义补足。

sentences.sentence: This first strategy for defiant children is ultimately the most important.
sentences.translation: 这一针对叛逆儿童的首要策略归根结底也是最重要的。

structure.text: This first strategy for defiant children
is
ultimately the most important

difficult_points: This first strategy 回指上一句所说的固定日常活动顺序。
ultimately 修饰判断，表示从根本或最终效果来看。

sentences.sentence: Developing a routine helps a child to know what to expect and increases the chances that he or she will comply with things such as chores, homework, and hygiene requests.
sentences.translation: 建立固定作息能帮助孩子知道接下来会发生什么，也能提高他们配合家务、作业和卫生要求等事项的可能性。

structure.text: Developing a routine
helps a child to know what to expect
and increases the chances that he or she will comply with things such as chores, homework, and hygiene requests

difficult_points: Developing a routine 是动名词短语作主语，helps 与 increases 是并列谓语。
what to expect 是疑问词加不定式，作 know 的宾语。
that he or she... 是同位语从句，说明 chances 的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: When undesirable activities occur in the same order at optimal times during the day, they become habits that are not questioned but done without thought.
sentences.translation: 当不受欢迎的活动每天都在合适的时间按相同顺序进行时，它们就会成为无需质疑、也不用思考便会完成的习惯。

structure.text: When undesirable activities occur in the same order at optimal times during the day
they become habits
that are not questioned but done without thought

difficult_points: When 从句给出活动成为习惯的条件和时间安排。
that 修饰 habits；not questioned but done 连接两个被动形式，第二个省略 are。

sentences.sentence: Chances are that you have developed some type of routine for yourself in terms of showering, cleaning your house, or doing other types of work.
sentences.translation: 你很可能已经为洗澡、打扫房屋或从事其他工作建立了某种固定程序。

structure.text: Chances are
that you have developed some type of routine for yourself
in terms of showering, cleaning your house, or doing other types of work

difficult_points: Chances are that... 是固定句式，表示“很可能……”。
showering、cleaning 和 doing 是三个并列动名词，说明 routine 涉及的事项。

sentences.sentence: You have an idea in your mind when you will do these things on a regular basis and this helps you to know what to expect.
sentences.translation: 你心里清楚自己通常会在什么时候做这些事，而这能帮助你预知接下来会发生什么。

structure.text: You have an idea in your mind
when you will do these things on a regular basis
and this helps you to know what to expect

difficult_points: when 从句说明 idea 的具体内容，即做这些事的时间安排。
this 回指前半句所说的心中有固定安排；what to expect 作 know 的宾语。

expressions.meaning_zh: 知道接下来会发生什么

sentences.sentence: In fact, you have probably already been using most of these compliance strategies for yourself without realizing it.
sentences.translation: 事实上，你很可能早已在不知不觉中把这些促进服从的策略大多用在自己身上了。

structure.text: In fact
you have probably already been using most of these compliance strategies for yourself
without realizing it

difficult_points: have been using 是现在完成进行时，强调从过去持续至今的使用。
without realizing it 表示没有意识到这一点，it 回指使用这些策略这件事。

expressions.meaning_zh: 在没有意识到的情况下

sentences.sentence: For children, without setting these expectations on a daily basis by making them part of a regular routine, they can become very upset.
sentences.translation: 对孩子来说，如果不通过把这些要求纳入固定作息来每天建立预期，他们就可能变得非常烦躁。

structure.text: For children
without setting these expectations on a daily basis by making them part of a regular routine
they can become very upset

difficult_points: without setting... 表示否定条件；by making... 说明建立预期的方式。
从严格语法看，without 短语的逻辑主语会被理解为 they，但语意实际是照护者未替孩子建立预期，原文存在悬垂修饰。

sentences.sentence: Just like adults, children think about what they plan to do that day and expect to be able to do what they want.
sentences.translation: 孩子和成年人一样，会考虑当天打算做什么，并期待自己能够做想做的事。

structure.text: Just like adults
children think about what they plan to do that day
and expect to be able to do what they want

difficult_points: children 同时支配 think about 和 expect 两个并列谓语。
两个 what 从句分别表示计划做的事和想做的事，what 在从句中作 do 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: So, when you come along and ask them to do something they weren't already planning to do that day, this can result in automatic refusals and other undesirable defiant behaviors.
sentences.translation: 所以，当你过来要求他们做当天原本没有计划的事时，这很可能引发本能的拒绝和其他不良的反抗行为。

structure.text: when you come along and ask them to do something they weren't already planning to do that day
this can result in automatic refusals and other undesirable defiant behaviors
they weren't already planning to do that day

difficult_points: when 从句描述触发拒绝的情境，this 回指整个情境。
something 后省略关系代词 that；they weren't... 修饰 something。

sentences.sentence: However, by using this compliance strategy with defiant children, these activities are done almost every day in the same general order and the child expects to already do them.
sentences.translation: 然而，把这种促进服从的策略用于叛逆儿童后，这些活动几乎每天都会按大致相同的顺序完成，孩子也会预期自己本来就要做这些事。

structure.text: However
by using this compliance strategy with defiant children
these activities are done almost every day in the same general order
and the child expects to already do them

difficult_points: by using... 表示实现后述结果的手段，但其逻辑施事者在被动主句中未明说。
them 指 these activities；expects to already do 表示孩子已把做这些事纳入预期。

sentences.sentence: Doctor Steven Walson addressed that organizing fun activities to occur after frequently refused activities.
sentences.translation: 史蒂文·沃尔森博士提出，可以把有趣的活动安排在孩子经常拒绝的活动之后。

structure.text: Doctor Steven Walson addressed
that organizing fun activities to occur after frequently refused activities

difficult_points: that 后只有动名词短语，没有完整谓语，原文宾语从句不完整。
organizing...to occur... 表示安排有趣活动在常被拒绝的活动之后进行，翻译按建议语气补足。

sentences.sentence: This strategy also works as a positive reinforcer when the child complies with your requests.
sentences.translation: 当孩子按你的要求去做时，这一策略也能起到积极强化物的作用。

structure.text: This strategy also works as a positive reinforcer
when the child complies with your requests

difficult_points: This strategy 回指把偏好活动安排在被拒活动之后的做法。
when 从句既表示发生时间，也隐含策略发挥作用的条件。

sentences.sentence: By arranging your day so that things often refused to occur right before highly preferred activities, you are able to eliminate defiant behavior and motivate your child's behavior of doing the undesirable activity.
sentences.translation: 通过安排一天的活动，让经常遭拒的事情恰好发生在孩子非常喜欢的活动之前，你就能减少反抗行为，并促使孩子完成不喜欢的活动。

structure.text: By arranging your day
so that things often refused to occur right before highly preferred activities
you are able to eliminate defiant behavior and motivate your child's behavior of doing the undesirable activity

difficult_points: By arranging... 表示手段，so that 引出安排希望达到的活动顺序。
原文 things often refused to occur 结构歧义，按上下文应指“经常遭拒的事情被安排在偏好活动之前”。
eliminate 与 motivate 是并列动词。

sentences.sentence: This is not to be presented in a way that the preferred activity is only allowed if a defiant child does the non-preferred activity.
sentences.translation: 但不应把这种安排说成：叛逆的孩子只有完成不喜欢的活动，才获准进行喜欢的活动。

structure.text: This is not to be presented
in a way that the preferred activity is only allowed
if a defiant child does the non-preferred activity

difficult_points: This 回指上一句的活动排序策略；is not to be presented 表示“不应被表述为”。
that 从句说明呈现方式，if 从句进一步限定偏好活动获准的条件。

expressions.text: be presented in a way that

sentences.sentence: However, you can word your request in a way so that your child assumes that you have to do the non-preferred activity before moving on to the next preferred activity.
sentences.translation: 不过，你可以换一种措辞，让孩子认为在进行下一个喜欢的活动前，必须先完成不喜欢的活动。

structure.text: you can word your request in a way
so that your child assumes
that you have to do the non-preferred activity before moving on to the next preferred activity

difficult_points: so that 引出措辞产生的预期效果，that 从句作 assumes 的宾语。
原文 you have to do 按上下文逻辑应指 child 必须做，存在人称指代不一致。

sentences.sentence: For example, you do not want to say something such as, "If you clean your room we can play a game."
sentences.translation: 例如，你不应该这样说：“如果你把房间打扫干净，我们就可以玩游戏。”

structure.text: For example
you do not want to say something such as
If you clean your room we can play a game.

difficult_points: something such as 后面的引语是被列为不推荐的表达示例。
引语内部 If 从句提出条件，主句 we can play a game 给出结果。

sentences.sentence: Instead of the word your request like this, "As soon as you are done cleaning your room we will be able to play that really fun game you wanted to play."
sentences.translation: 原文想表达的是：应改成这样提出要求：“你一打扫完房间，我们就可以玩你想玩的那个特别有趣的游戏了。”

structure.text: Instead of the word your request like this
As soon as you are done cleaning your room we will be able to play that really fun game you wanted to play.

difficult_points: Instead of the word your request like this 语法残缺，结合上下文应理解为“相反，要这样措辞”。
引语中 As soon as 引导时间从句；you wanted to play 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 game。

sentences.sentence: Psychologist Paul Edith insisted praise is the best way to make children comply with.
sentences.translation: 心理学家保罗·伊迪丝坚持认为，表扬是促使孩子服从的最佳方式。

structure.text: Psychologist Paul Edith insisted
praise is the best way to make children comply with

difficult_points: insisted 后省略 that，后面整个分句是其宾语。
comply with 通常必须带宾语，原文句末缺少介词宾语；翻译按“让孩子服从”处理。

sentences.sentence: This is probably a common term you are used to hearing by now.
sentences.translation: 到现在，这大概已经是一个你听惯了的常见说法。

structure.text: This is probably a common term
you are used to hearing by now

difficult_points: term 后省略关系代词 that，you are used to hearing... 修饰 term。
be used to 后接动名词 hearing，表示“习惯于听到”，不是“被用于”。

sentences.sentence: If you praise your child's behavior, he or she will be more likely to do that behavior.
sentences.translation: 如果你表扬孩子的某种行为，孩子今后就更可能再次这样做。

structure.text: If you praise your child's behavior
he or she will be more likely to do that behavior

difficult_points: If 从句给出条件，主句用 will 表示可能产生的后续结果。
he or she 指 your child；do that behavior 表示重复受到表扬的行为。

sentences.sentence: So, it is essential to use praise when working with defiant children.
sentences.translation: 因此，在引导叛逆儿童时，运用表扬至关重要。

structure.text: So
it is essential
to use praise
when working with defiant children

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，真正主语是 to use praise。
when working... 是省略主语和 be 动词的时间／情境从句，逻辑主语为照护者。

sentences.sentence: It also provides your child with positive attention.
sentences.translation: 表扬也能让孩子获得积极的关注。

structure.text: It
also provides
your child with positive attention

difficult_points: It 回指上一句的 use praise，即运用表扬。
provide someone with something 表示“向某人提供某物”。

sentences.sentence: However, it is important to know how to praise children in a way that encourages future automatic reinforcement for your child when doing a similar behavior.
sentences.translation: 不过，重要的是要懂得如何恰当地表扬孩子，使孩子日后做出类似行为时能形成自动的自我强化。

structure.text: However
it is important
to know how to praise children
in a way that encourages future automatic reinforcement for your child when doing a similar behavior

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，to know... 是真正主语；how to praise 作 know 的宾语。
that 引导定语从句修饰 way；when doing... 的逻辑主语按语意是 child，而非句法最近的施事者。
